_readme: Worked example for `dark-factory init` (see references/authoring.md). Edit workspace_root, control_root, and builder_adapter below to real absolute paths on your machine before running init -- this field is documentation only and is ignored by df_init/init.
spec_text:
# KV Store — a small JSON HTTP API (Python standard library only)

Build a tiny in-memory key/value store exposed as a JSON HTTP API, in a
single file `app.py`, using ONLY the Python standard library (no
third-party packages).

## Required internal interface (this is how your code is exercised)

Factor ALL request handling into one pure function:

    handle(method: str, path: str, body: str | None) -> tuple[int, str]

- `method` is the HTTP method ("GET", "PUT", "DELETE", ...).
- `path` is the request path (e.g. "/kv/color").
- `body` is the raw request body as a string, or None when there is no body.
- It returns `(status_code, response_body)` where `response_body` is a JSON
  string.
- `handle` uses a module-level dict as the store, so repeated calls within
  one process share state (a PUT then a GET in the same process sees the
  value).

Also provide `main()` that starts a `http.server` on a `--port <N>`
argument and dispatches every request through `handle(...)`. Running
`python3 app.py --port 0` must start the server (binding an ephemeral port
is fine). The HTTP layer must be a thin wrapper over `handle`.

## Behaviors

1. **Health**: `GET /health` -> `200`, body `{"status": "ok"}`.
2. **Put**: `PUT /kv/<key>` with body `{"value": <any JSON>}` -> `200`, body
   `{"key": "<key>", "value": <value>}`, and the value is stored under
   `<key>`.
3. **Get**: `GET /kv/<key>` -> `200` body `{"key": "<key>", "value": <value>}`
   if the key exists; otherwise `404` body `{"error": "not found"}`.
4. **List**: `GET /kv` -> `200` body `{"keys": [<sorted list of stored keys>]}`.
5. **Delete**: `DELETE /kv/<key>` -> `200` body `{"deleted": "<key>"}` if it
   existed (and it is removed); otherwise `404` body `{"error": "not found"}`.
6. **Validation**: `PUT /kv/<key>` with a body that is not valid JSON, or
   valid JSON lacking a `"value"` field -> `400` body `{"error": "<message>"}`.
7. **Unknown route**: any path that is not `/health`, `/kv`, or `/kv/<key>`
   -> `404` body `{"error": "not found"}`.

Response bodies must be JSON. Keys in the `/kv` listing are sorted
ascending.

scenarios.title: health check returns 200 ok
run:
import json,sys,app
st,b=0,''
for op in json.loads(sys.argv[1]):
    st,b=app.handle(op[0],op[1],op[2] if len(op)>2 else None)
print(st)
print(b)

scenarios.title: health check ignores an unexpected request body

st,b=0,''

    st,b=app.handle(op[0],op[1],op[2] if len(op)>2 else None)

behaviors.description: PUT /kv/<key> stores a value and returns 200

scenarios.title: put a new key returns 200 and echoes it
run:
import json,sys,app
st,b=0,''
for op in json.loads(sys.argv[1]):
    st,b=app.handle(op[0],op[1],op[2] if len(op)>2 else None)
print(st)

scenarios.title: round-trip property: any generated key/value comes back from a get
scenarios.given: M43a worked example -- put then get in ONE process (module-level store); the stored value must appear in the get response, for every generated pair

run:
import json,sys,app
st,b=0,''
for op in json.loads(sys.argv[1]):
    st,b=app.handle(op[0],op[1],op[2] if len(op)>2 else None)
print(st)
print(b)

behaviors.description: GET /kv/<key> returns the stored value, or 404 if absent

scenarios.title: get after put returns the stored value

scenarios.title: fuzz property: handle() never crashes on malformed PUT bodies
scenarios.given: M43a worked example -- adversarial variants of a valid body (bit-flip/truncate/oversize/wrong-type/control-chars/injection/empty) are fed as the raw body; a correct app answers 400 per the spec, and must NEVER raise

scenarios.title: unknown route returns 404
run:
import json,sys,app
st,b=0,''
for op in json.loads(sys.argv[1]):
    st,b=app.handle(op[0],op[1],op[2] if len(op)>2 else None)
print(st)
print(b)
